headline: Iceland Outlook Revised To Positive On Strengthening Fiscal Position; 'A+/A-1' Ratings Affirmed
summary: On March 6, 2026, S&P Global Ratings revised the outlook on its long-term ratings on Iceland topositive from stable. At the same time, the long- and short-term foreign and localcurrency sovereign credit ratings were affirmed at 'A+/A-1'. The positive outlook primarily reflects the potential for Iceland’s budgetary performance tostrengthen over the next two years, leading to a further sustained decline in net generalgovernment debt. headline: 3 Reasons RTX is Risky and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: Since March 2021, the S&P 500 has delivered a total return of 79.5%. But one standout stock has more than doubled the market - over the past five years, RTX has surged 170% to $204.06 per share. Its momentum hasn’t stopped as it’s also gained 32.3% in the last six months thanks to its solid quarterly results, beating the S&P by 26.7%.

headline: Are Oil Stocks Due for a Drop? What the Charts Say.
summary: Crude oil has rallied sharply in recent sessions with  jumping almost 25% for the week amid escalating Middle East tensions, as investors position for potential supply disruptions.  Strength in the energy sector was evident well before the recent escalations in the Middle East.  Demonstrating its consistency, it not fallen in consecutive sessions for two months.
